steps.title: Find what the blank is doing in the sentence
steps.content: The blank is the subject of "have a special genetic ability called RNA editing." That subject is "cephalopods," followed by an appositive ("ocean dwellers that include the squid, the octopus, and the cuttlefish") that defines what cephalopods are.

steps.title: Apply the matched-punctuation rule for nonrestrictive appositives
steps.content: A nonrestrictive appositive that interrupts a sentence must be set off on BOTH sides by matching punctuation (two commas or two dashes). Because the appositive already contains internal commas (squid, octopus, cuttlefish), dashes are the clean choice for the outer pair.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cephalopods, ocean dwellers that include the squid, the octopus, and the cuttlefish: opens the appositive with a comma but never closes it before "have" — unmatched punctuation. ✗
  • Choice B — cephalopods — ocean dwellers — that include the squid, the octopus, and the cuttlefish,: dashes wrongly chop "ocean dwellers" off from its own modifier "that include...," and the trailing comma before "have" wrongly splits subject from verb. ✗
  • Choice C — cephalopods, ocean dwellers that include : the squid, the octopus, and the cuttlefish,: a colon cannot follow "include" (no independent clause precedes it), and the trailing comma again splits subject from verb. ✗
  • Choice D — cephalopods — ocean dwellers that include the squid, the octopus, and the cuttlefish —: matched dashes correctly enclose the whole appositive, leaving "cephalopods... have" intact. ✓
D is correct.
